title: Test transformations like production code
description: Attach data tests to models so uniqueness, referential integrity, and freshness assumptions fail loudly before consumers see bad data.
body: Use when a model's output feeds dashboards or downstream models. Do not ship transformations untested on the assumption that upstream data is clean — a broken join silently fans out wrong numbers everywhere. Actions: add tests for primary-key uniqueness and not-null, referential integrity to parent keys, accepted values, and source freshness; run them in CI and on each build. Validation signals include a CI run executing model tests and a failing test blocking a build on seeded bad data. Risk boundary: untested transformations propagate errors warehouse-wide; if a model lacks key and relationship tests, treat it as unsafe to publish and add them before exposing it.
